summary: Codex-only post-ingest scope was limited to completed report-backed waves. Wave 14 was observed as an active separate ingest process and was deferred without babysitting or intervention.

Deterministic post-ingest manifests were generated for completed waves 1, 2, 3, 11, and 13. Together they cover 1050 selected sources and 42123 related canonical records in the current materialized corpus. The large unprocessed backlog is wave 11 with 213 completed and 7 quarantined ingests, plus wave 13 with 636 completed and 64 quarantined ingests.

The completed-wave manifests all report the same current global validation snapshot: 1437 validation issues, including 1196 duplicate_global_identity issues. In-scope identity-review suggestions are present but parse_error-marked across the completed waves, so this Codex-only pass did not merge, distinguish, autoaccept, or apply identity decisions.

The first Codex-only writer pass should prioritize durable synthesis on high-value bus-priority project, corridor, and route pages rather than filling every empty writer region. Wave 11 is bus-priority-heavy and is suitable for targeted prose. Wave 13 is mostly broader MTA meeting material, so most wave 13 records should remain canonical/searchable context unless they directly support a bus-priority page or reveal a data-quality issue.
